drafting_legal_documents.element: Drafting pleadings, motions, or legal documents

court_communications.element: Communicating with court on behalf of another

representation.element: Appearing or acting as representative in legal proceedings

conspiracy.element: Conspiracy to commit UPL + deprive civil rights

albert_watson_statement.statement: NS2505044 — Albert Watson told police: 'they want this incident documented so Emily can go tomorrow to get an Ex Parte order'
albert_watson_statement.significance: CASE KILLER — proves Berry and Emily premeditated the abuse of court process
legal_impact: Proves ex parte order was pre-planned, not emergency
Pierces judicial immunity for co-conspirators (Dennis v Sparks 449 US 24)

who_said_what.albert_watson: Emily's father-in-law, told police about the plan
who_said_what.berry_role: Orchestrated the plan to get ex parte order
who_said_what.emily_role: Carried out the plan by filing false ex parte motion

authorities.rule: Unauthorized practice of law — misdemeanor (up to $1,000 fine, 1 year jail)
Each act of UPL is a separate offense
Criminal conspiracy — same penalty as underlying offense
Federal civil rights — Berry as state actor through conspiracy with court
Private parties who conspire with state actors lose immunity
Michigan definition of 'practice of law' — includes document drafting

authorities.cite: Surety Ins Co v Williams 729 F2d 581
authorities.rule: Non-attorney who drafts court documents engages in UPL
Only licensed attorneys may practice law in Michigan courts

referral_package.recipient: State Bar of Michigan, Unauthorized Practice of Law Committee
referral_package.subject: Complaint: Ronald T. Berry — Unauthorized Practice of Law
specific_acts: Drafted legal motions filed in 14th Circuit Court case 2024-001507-DC
Communicated with Friend of Court (Pamela Rusco) on Emily Watson's behalf
Orchestrated ex parte strategy documented in police report NS2505044
Provided legal advice and directed Emily Watson's litigation strategy
Prepared documents that were signed and filed by Emily Watson
referral_package.criminal_referral: Recommend referral to Muskegon County Prosecutor under MCL 600.916

recommendation: File State Bar complaint + include UPL in federal 1983 complaint + criminal referral to prosecutor
